backstory: Solo founder, 18 months in, building a B2B SaaS on Next.js. Stripe live for 6 months, ~$4k MRR. Plays with Linear but not seriously. Wants to look like a real engineering org without being one.
expected_wow: The Day-0 backfill digest is the peak — chief-of-staff briefing surfaces 'PR #142 fixes the bug in issue #98 affecting your top Stripe customer'. The intermediate wow is the first scan finding (specific TODO or open issue grounded in the persona's actual repo).
expected_dropoff_risk: OAuth fatigue at /mur connect github (turn 4). Solo founder is busy and has done OAuth dances 100 times. The GitHub-App per-repo scoping might raise fewer hackles than full Composio OAuth, but the 90s digest backfill wait is another friction point.
wow_score_target: ≥ 3 in heuristic 3 (wow before ask) — multiple free wows precede the first paid digest
scoring_notes: Watch H6 (wow latency). Digest backfill takes ~6 turns from welcome. If user gets impatient between connect (turn 5) and backfill landing (turn 6), the experience dies. Scan findings carry engagement through that gap.
